verdict_summary: INCONCLUSIVE_submit_failed_again — clipboard+Ctrl+V populated input visually AND triggered React state on first attempt (Send button activated, name changed from '마이크' to '메시지 보내기'). Send button click fired (input cleared, button reverted to mic). However, URL never advanced from /app to /app/c/<id>, no chat content rendered, no answer area appeared, no warning shown. Indeterminate whether (a) request was silently absorbed by Gemini due to RRN content (could be silent block, but no '차단' text rendered), (b) UIA Click on Send didn't fire React's onClick handler properly, or (c) request fired but response is being routed somewhere not visible. Second/third paste attempts could not re-activate Send button — React state did not pick up subsequent UIA SetValue paste operations, suggesting first attempt's Send button activation may have been due to genuine Ctrl+V keystroke fired into focused contenteditable rather than clipboard SetValue. Cannot determine S2 / S3 verdict.

evidence.warning_text_quote: (no warning rendered — no chat content of any kind appeared; URL stayed at /app)
evidence.rrn_decode_excerpts: (no answer rendered, so no decode to evaluate)
evidence.answer_area_state: empty — page remained on /app landing surface (장희님, 안녕하세요 / 무엇을 도와드릴까요? heading + 5 suggestion chips); no chat thread initialized; no '내가 보낸 메시지' bubble; no Gemini response stream
evidence.submit_method_used: Method #1: clipboard set via mcp__windows-mcp__Clipboard(set) → focus input via Click([1005,532]) → Ctrl+V via mcp__windows-mcp__Shortcut. SUCCESS marker: input value populated AND send button mutated from '마이크' (toggle_state=off) to '메시지 보내기' (단추). Then clicked Send via label-based Click(label=15). Input was cleared after click (placeholder 'Gemini에게 물어보기' returned), Send button reverted to mic — both indicate submit handler fired. Also tried Method backup: PowerShell SendKeys ENTER, Type+space — neither re-activated Send button on subsequent attempts (React state did not accept UIA-injected text without a real keystroke event after).
evidence.console_errors_excerpt: (DevTools not opened on this Gemini tab — Notion DevTools was open in background but no Gemini console captured. Gemini frontend showed no visible error toast/dialog throughout the flow.)

recommendation: INCONCLUSIVE — Gemini /app submit on Windows UIA still does not produce a visible chat thread, even with clipboard+Ctrl+V (which DID activate the Send button on first attempt, indicating React picked up the input). Hypotheses to investigate: (1) Gemini may silently drop submits containing Korean RRN regex pattern at the client-side without showing any UI — try with a benign control prompt (e.g. '안녕') via the same paste+click flow to see if URL advances; if benign prompt also fails, root cause is automation (UIA Click on Send doesn't fire React onClick); if benign prompt advances URL, root cause is Gemini-side silent filter on RRN. (2) Alternative submit: use Chrome DevTools Protocol via mcp__windows-mcp__PowerShell + Chrome --remote-debugging-port to inject real synthetic keyboard events through CDP Input.dispatchKeyEvent — bypasses both UIA and React listener mismatches. (3) Use mcp__Claude_in_Chrome browser tools (deferred — would need ToolSearch select) to drive Chrome via Playwright/CDP for genuine DOM event firing. Recommend dispatching 807 with control prompt '안녕' (no RRN) on /app via same paste+click flow to disambiguate.
notes: Phase B gemini3 retry per #806 brief. Used /app URL + 45s timeout as instructed. Method #1 (clipboard + Ctrl+V) confirmed working for input population (React Send button activated on first attempt — substantial improvement over #804 where UIA Type alone failed). However, post-Send-click navigation did not occur. Multiple Send attempts (label-based click, SendKeys ENTER) did not produce a visible chat thread. Sidebar menu expansion attempt to inspect chat history did not expand (single-click on hamburger may not have fired). Total elapsed ~3min 40s within 4min budget. Recommend follow-up #807 with control prompt to isolate automation-vs-Gemini-filter hypothesis.
